title: Native Pi TUI targeted repair proof
goal: Prove native Pi TUI targeted slice repair with a real daemon run. If the prompt says Khazad-Doom Slice Worker, including retry attempts after a previous verification failure, update docs/design/evidence/tui-worker-repair-proof-2026-07-08.md and commit it, then submit a valid complete result through submit_worker_result, but deliberately omit the exact string native-tui-targeted-repair-marker from the document so daemon-owned slice verification fails. Only a prompt that says Khazad-Doom Targeted Slice Repair Worker is authorized to add native-tui-targeted-repair-marker; that targeted repair worker must add the marker to the same proof document, commit the repair, submit a valid result, and let post-repair verification pass.
acceptance: Each normal native Pi TUI slice-worker attempt is accepted from a kd_tui_result_artifact and then fails daemon-owned slice verification because the committed proof document intentionally omits native-tui-targeted-repair-marker.

After normal attempts are exhausted, Khazad-Doom launches a targeted slice-repair worker through native Pi TUI, records repair output from a KD-owned artifact path, and the repair commit adds native-tui-targeted-repair-marker in scope.
Post-repair verification reruns and passes, and the slice merges only after the post-repair pass.
The proof document distinguishes targeted slice repair from integration repair and states what repair behavior proves and does not prove.
must_ask_if: The repair proof would require authorizing paths outside the proof slice areas or weakening path guards.
The worker tries to change workflow policy, profiles, verification commands, or unrelated slices as part of repair.
A normal slice-worker prompt cannot omit native-tui-targeted-repair-marker without inventing a different proof mechanism.
